name: content-research-writer
description: Assists in writing high-quality content by conducting research, adding citations, improving hooks, iterating on outlines, and providing real-time feedback on each section. Transforms your writing process from solo effort to collaborative partnership.
capabilities: Creates structured outlines with research placeholders and logical flow
Conducts research on topics and adds proper citations
Improves article hooks and introductions for better engagement
Provides section-by-section feedback on clarity, flow, and evidence
Preserves writer's voice and style across revisions
Manages citations and references in multiple formats
author: DYAI2025
securitySummary: This is a prompt-based writing assistance skill containing only markdown documentation. Static findings are false positives: shell command examples are user setup instructions, source URL is metadata, and content research references are legitimate use cases, not system reconnaissance. No executable code, scripts, network operations, or file system access beyond standard AI capabilities.
valueStatement: Writing quality content takes significant effort. This skill transforms solo writing into a collaborative partnership by providing research assistance, structured outlines, real-time section feedback, and proper citation management.
limitations: Does not write full articles from scratch without user input
Does not access paid databases or subscription-only sources
Does not verify facts beyond web search capabilities
Cannot publish or upload content to external platforms

useCases.title: Write blog posts faster
useCases.description: Create outlines, research key points, and get section-by-section feedback to publish quality articles efficiently.

useCases.title: Document with citations
useCases.description: Write technical documentation with proper research, citations, and clear explanations for complex topics.

useCases.title: Develop compelling narratives
useCases.description: Craft opinion pieces and thought leadership content with strong hooks, evidence, and distinctive voice.

promptTemplates.title: Create outline
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create an outline for an article about [topic]. Ask me about my target audience, length, and writing goals first.

promptTemplates.title: Research topic
promptTemplates.prompt: Research [specific topic] and add citations to my outline. Find recent data and credible sources I can reference.

promptTemplates.title: Improve hook
promptTemplates.prompt: Here's my introduction: [paste text]. Analyze it and provide 3 alternative hooks with explanations of why each works.

promptTemplates.title: Get feedback
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this section I just wrote: [paste section]. Give me specific feedback on clarity, flow, and suggestions for improvement.

bestPractices: Work on one section at a time and get feedback before moving on to maintain momentum.
Save research separately in a research.md file to keep your draft focused and clean.
Specify your voice and style preferences early so the skill can match your tone throughout.
antiPatterns: Do not expect the skill to write your entire article without your input on each section.
Avoid skipping the outlining phase, as it saves time and improves structure later.
Do not ignore feedback on citations, as unverifiable claims weaken your credibility.

faq.question: What AI tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for writing assistance.

faq.question: Can it access paid research databases?
faq.answer: No. The skill searches publicly available web sources. For subscription-only research, provide your own sources.

faq.question: How does it handle citations?
faq.answer: It supports inline citations, numbered references, and footnotes. It maintains a running reference list for your article.

faq.question: Is my writing data safe?
faq.answer: This is a prompt-based skill with no data storage. Your content stays within your Claude session.

faq.question: Why is my outline not being generated?
faq.answer: The skill asks clarifying questions first. Answer them to get a tailored outline for your specific needs.

faq.question: How is this different from a basic writing assistant?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured workflows, research integration, section-by-section iterative feedback, and voice preservation across revisions.
